aggregation.group_key_encoding: Map returnflag to index: 'A'->0, 'N'->1, 'R'->2. Map linestatus: 'F'->0, 'O'->1. Compact key = rf_idx * 2 + ls_idx. Use a 256-byte lookup table for each.
aggregation.accumulator_notes: CRITICAL: Use double for ALL sum fields — NOT long double. The previous iteration incorrectly used long double in the Acc struct despite the plan saying double. long double forces the x87 FPU which prevents SSE/AVX vectorization and is 2-3x slower. The struct must be: struct Acc { double sum_qty; double sum_base_price; double sum_disc_price; double sum_charge; double sum_discount; int64_t count; }; — exactly 48 bytes.

data_structures.mmap_strategy: Do NOT use MAP_POPULATE. Use plain MAP_PRIVATE only. After mmap, call madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) to enable kernel readahead. MAP_POPULATE forces synchronous page table population (99ms overhead on hot runs when pages are already in cache). Without it, pages fault lazily during the sequential scan, overlapping with computation.

parallelism.merge: Iterate only 6 entries per thread. Total merge work: 6 × nthreads iterations.
parallelism.notes: Each thread has a stack-allocated array of 6 Acc structs (double, not long double). The inner loop should be tight enough for auto-vectorization: load 4 doubles, compute disc_price and charge, accumulate.

output.format_notes: Reverse-map compact index back to char: rf_chars[]={'A','N','R'}, ls_chars[]={'F','O'}.

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category A+C): Two issues. (1) MAP_POPULATE in mmap causes 99ms of synchronous page table population on hot runs — 69% of total time — even though pages are already in page cache. Fix: remove MAP_POPULATE, use plain MAP_PRIVATE with MADV_SEQUENTIAL to let kernel handle readahead lazily during scan. (2) The Acc struct used long double despite the plan specifying double, forcing x87 FPU and preventing SSE/AVX vectorization. Fix: enforce double accumulators (48 bytes per entry) for SSE-friendly inner loop. Expected improvement: data_loading drops from 99ms to near-zero (merged into scan), scan may increase slightly but net saving ~80-90ms.
